title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot and his companion discuss the disappearance of Miss Fay at the Blaux Han Hotel. Luca Lazari, a hotel representative, attempts to distract Poirot with descriptions of the hotel's luxury, but Poirot remains focused on the three recent murders. The victims, Mrs. Harriet Sipper, Miss Edith Gransbury, and Mr. Richard Nigges, were all guests who checked into the hotel separately on Tuesday and were found dead in their locked rooms the following day.

The narrator notes that the victims were killed alone in their respective rooms and that the room keys are missing, leading to the suspicion that the killer took them. Poirot suggests the killer may have taken the keys along with several cufflinks. The cause of death appears to be cyanide poisoning, administered through tea consumed by Sipper and Gransbury, and sherry consumed by Nigges. Forensic examinations place the time of death between 7:15 PM and 8:20 PM.

Lazari claims that at 7:15 PM, all three victims were seen by staff when their room service food was delivered. Poirot finds the coincidence of three separate guests checking in on the same day, dying on the same day, and all requesting room service at the exact same time to be impossible. To investigate these discrepancies, Poirot demands that Lazari gather all hotel staff in an empty room for questioning. Meanwhile, Poirot and the narrator proceed to the victims' rooms to conduct a thorough search.
